name: Greek Meatballs (Keftedes)
content:
# Greek Meatballs (Keftedes)

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/greek-meatballs/)

**Cuisine:** Greek | **Type:** Meatballs / Appetizer / Main | **Method:** Stovetop

**Prep Time:** 20 mins | **Cook Time:** 15 mins | **Total Time:** 35 mins

**Yield:** Makes about 25 meatballs (serves 4-5)

---

## Description

These are the most incredible Greek meatballs — crispy on the outside, juicy and tender on the inside, packed with herbs and flavors of the Mediterranean. Authentic keftedes are a staple in Greek cuisine, served as mezze, over rice, or stuffed into pita bread. The combination of oregano, mint, and lemon zest makes these truly special.

---

## Ingredients

- 500g / 1 lb beef mince (ground beef)
- 250g / 8 oz pork mince (ground pork)
- 1 small onion, very finely grated
- 3 garlic cloves, minced
- 1/4 cup fresh breadcrumbs
- 1 egg
- 2 tbsp fresh mint, finely chopped
- 1 tbsp dried oregano
- Zest of 1 lemon
- 1 tsp ground cumin
- 1 tsp salt
- 1/2 tsp black pepper
- 2 tbsp olive oil (for pan-frying)
- Plain flour for dusting

### For Serving:
- Tzatziki sauce
- Lemon wedges
- Warm pita bread
- Greek salad

---

## Instructions

1. **Prepare the mixture:** In a large bowl, combine the beef mince, pork mince, grated onion, garlic, breadcrumbs, egg, fresh mint, oregano, lemon zest, cumin, salt, and pepper. Mix well with your hands until everything is evenly combined. Do not overmix.

2. **Rest the mixture:** Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes (or up to overnight). This helps the flavors meld and makes the meatballs easier to shape.

3. **Shape the meatballs:** Using damp hands, scoop about 1.5 tablespoons of mixture and roll into balls roughly the size of a golf ball. You should get about 25 meatballs. Lightly dust each meatball with flour — this helps create a crispy exterior.

4. **Heat the oil:** Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering.

5. **Cook the meatballs:** Working in batches, add meatballs to the pan in a single layer. Cook for about 2-3 minutes per side, turning occasionally, until golden brown on all sides and cooked through (internal temp 71°C / 160°F). This should take about 8-10 minutes total. Do not overcrowd the pan.

6. **Rest and serve:** Transfer cooked meatballs to a plate lined with paper towels to drain excess oil. Season lightly with extra salt. Serve hot with tzatziki, lemon wedges, and warm pita bread.

---

## Tips

- **Don't skip the flour dusting** — it creates that signature crispy crust that makes keftedes special.
- **Finely grate the onion** rather than chopping it — this releases moisture and helps keep the meatballs tender.
- **Use a mix of beef and pork** for the best flavor and juiciness. If you must use only beef, add an extra egg yolk for moisture.
- **Make ahead:** Uncooked meatballs can be frozen on a baking sheet, then transferred to a freezer bag for up to 3 months. Cook from frozen, adding a few extra minutes.
- **Alternative cooking method:** These can also be baked at 200°C (400°F) for about 20 minutes, turning halfway through. They won't be quite as crispy but are a healthier option.

---

## Nutrition (per meatball, approx.)

| Nutrient | Amount |
|----------|--------|
| Calories | 75 kcal |
| Protein | 6g |
| Fat | 5g |
| Carbohydrates | 2g |
| Fiber | 0g |
| Sodium | 110mg |

name: Crispy Sticky Mongolian Beef
content:
# Crispy Sticky Mongolian Beef

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/crispy-sticky-mongolian-beef/)

**Cuisine:** Chinese | **Type:** Beef / Stir-Fry / Main | **Method:** Stir-Fry

**Prep Time:** 15 mins | **Cook Time:** 10 mins | **Total Time:** 30 mins (plus marinating)

**Yield:** Serves 4

---

## Description

Better-than-takeout Mongolian Beef that's secretly easy to make at home! The beef is coated in a light, crispy cornstarch batter before being pan-fried to golden perfection, then tossed in a glossy, savory-sweet sauce. The secret is the crispy-then-saucy technique — the beef gets beautifully crispy before being coated in the sticky sauce, giving you texture AND flavor in every bite.

---

## Ingredients

### For the Beef:
- 500g / 1 lb flank steak or sirloin, sliced thinly against the grain
- 1/4 cup cornstarch (for coating)
- 1/4 tsp salt
- 1/4 tsp black pepper
- Oil for shallow frying (about 1/3 cup)

### For the Sauce:
- 1/3 cup soy sauce (low sodium preferred)
- 1/3 cup water

- 1/2 cup brown sugar (packed)
- 2 tbsp hoisin sauce
- 1 tbsp oyster sauce
- 1 tsp sesame oil

### For the Stir-Fry:
- 4 garlic cloves, minced
- 1 tbsp fresh ginger, grated
- 4-5 dried red chili peppers (or 1/2 tsp chili flakes)
- 3 spring onions (scallions), cut into 2-inch pieces

### Garnish:
- Sesame seeds
- Sliced spring onions

---

## Instructions

1. **Slice the beef:** Place the steak in the freezer for 20-30 minutes to firm up (makes slicing easier). Slice thinly against the grain, about 1/4 inch thick.

2. **Marinate:** Season the beef with salt and pepper. Toss with cornstarch until each piece is lightly and evenly coated. Let sit for 10 minutes.

3. **Make the sauce:** Whisk together soy sauce, water, brown sugar, hoisin sauce, oyster sauce, and sesame oil in a small bowl. Set aside.

4. **Fry the beef:** Heat oil in a large skillet or wok over high heat until shimmering. Working in batches, fry the beef in a single layer for 1-2 minutes per side until crispy and golden brown. Remove to a paper towel-lined plate. Repeat with remaining beef.

5. **Stir-fry aromatics:** Remove all but 1 tablespoon of oil from the pan. Add garlic, ginger, and dried chilies. Stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.

6. **Add the sauce:** Pour the sauce mixture into the pan and bring to a simmer. Cook for 1-2 minutes until the sauce thickens and becomes glossy.

7. **Toss beef in sauce:** Add the crispy beef back to the pan and toss to coat evenly in the sauce. Add the spring onion pieces and toss for another 30 seconds.

8. **Serve:** Transfer to a serving platter, garnish with sesame seeds and extra sliced spring onions. Serve immediately over steamed jasmine rice.

---

## Tips

- **Cornstarch coating is key** — it's what gives the beef that crispy texture that stays even after being coated in sauce.
- **Slice against the grain** for tender beef. Look for the direction of the muscle fibers and cut perpendicular to them.
- **Don't overcrowd when frying** — the beef will steam instead of crisp if the pan is too full. Work in 2-3 batches.
- **Make it spicy:** Add more dried chilies or a tablespoon of sriracha to the sauce for extra heat.
- **The sauce will thicken** as it cools, so serve immediately for the best texture.

---

## Nutrition (per serving, approx.)

| Nutrient | Amount |
|----------|--------|
| Calories | 380 kcal |
| Protein | 28g |
| Fat | 14g |
| Carbohydrates | 35g |
| Fiber | 1g |
| Sodium | 1200mg |

name: Nachos Pie
content:
# Nachos Pie

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/nachos-pie/)

**Cuisine:** Mexican | **Type:** Casserole / Main / Comfort Food | **Method:** Baked

**Prep Time:** 25 mins | **Cook Time:** 30 mins | **Total Time:** 55 mins

**Yield:** Serves 6

---

## Description

All the incredible flavors of loaded nachos, reimagined as a comforting, family-friendly pie! A crispy tortilla chip base is layered with seasoned beef, beans, cheese, and all the classic nacho toppings, then baked until golden and bubbly. It's a crowd-pleasing dish that's perfect for weeknight dinners, game day, or potlucks.

---

## Ingredients

### For the Filling:
- 500g / 1 lb beef mince (ground beef)
- 1 onion, diced
- 2 garlic cloves, minced
- 1 tsp chili powder
- 1 tsp ground cumin
- 1/2 tsp smoked paprika
- 1/2 tsp oregano
- 1 can (400g / 14 oz) red kidney beans, drained and rinsed
- 1 can (400g / 14 oz) crushed tomatoes
- 1/2 cup corn kernels (fresh, frozen, or canned)
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 1/4 tsp black pepper

### For the Assembly:
- 2 large bags tortilla chips (about 200g total)
- 2 cups shredded cheddar cheese
- 1 cup shredded mozzarella cheese

### For the Topping:
- Sour cream
- Sliced jalapeños
- Sliced spring onions (scallions)
- Fresh cilantro
- Diced avocado
- Hot sauce (optional)

---

## Instructions

1. **Preheat oven** to 190°C (375°F). Grease a 9-inch deep pie dish or springform pan.

2. **Cook the meat:** Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the beef mince and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned (about 5 minutes). Add onion and garlic, cook for 2 minutes until softened.

3. **Season and simmer:** Add chili powder, cumin, smoked paprika, and oregano. Stir well and cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Add the kidney beans, crushed tomatoes, corn, salt, and pepper. Bring to a simmer and cook for 5-8 minutes until slightly thickened.

4. **Layer the chips:** Spread half the tortilla chips evenly across the bottom of the prepared dish. Break some into smaller pieces to fill gaps.

5. **Layer the filling:** Spoon half the meat mixture over the chips. Sprinkle with half the cheddar and half the mozzarella. Repeat with remaining chips, meat mixture, and cheese.

6. **Bake:** Place the dish on a baking tray (to catch any drips). Bake for 20-25 minutes until the cheese is melted, golden, and bubbly.

7. **Rest:** Let the nachos pie rest for 5-10 minutes before slicing. This helps it hold its shape when serving.

8. **Top and serve:** Top with sour cream, jalapeños, spring onions, cilantro, and avocado. Slice into wedges and serve immediately.

---

## Tips

- **Use sturdy tortilla chips** that won't get too soggy — thicker, restaurant-style chips work best.
- **Don't skip the resting time** — it allows the layers to set slightly so slices hold together.
- **Make it vegetarian:** Replace the beef with an extra can of beans and some diced bell peppers.
- **Spice level:** Adjust the chili powder and add cayenne pepper to taste for more heat.
- **Make ahead:** Assemble the pie up to a few hours ahead and refrigerate. Add 5-10 minutes to the baking time if starting cold.

---

## Nutrition (per serving, approx.)

| Nutrient | Amount |
|----------|--------|
| Calories | 520 kcal |
| Protein | 32g |
| Fat | 26g |
| Carbohydrates | 40g |
| Fiber | 7g |
| Sodium | 890mg |

name: Fall Apart Mexican Beef Ribs
content:
# Fall Apart Mexican Beef Ribs

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/fall-apart-mexican-beef-ribs/)

**Cuisine:** Mexican | **Type:** Beef / Ribs / Main | **Method:** Stovetop

**Prep Time:** 15 mins | **Cook Time:** 2 hours 30 mins | **Total Time:** 2 hours 45 mins

**Yield:** Serves 4-6

---

## Description

Incredibly tender, fall-off-the-bone beef ribs braised low and slow in a rich, smoky, spicy Mexican-inspired sauce. The ribs become melt-in-your-mouth tender as they simmer in a sauce made with chipotle, ancho chili, cumin, and tomatoes. This is comfort food at its finest — impressive enough for guests but easy enough for a weekend family dinner.

---

## Ingredients

### For the Ribs:
- 1.5 kg / 3.3 lbs beef short ribs (bone-in)
- 1 tsp salt
- 1/2 tsp black pepper
- 2 tbsp vegetable oil

### For the Sauce:
- 1 large onion, diced
n- 4 garlic cloves, minced
- 2 tbsp chipotle peppers in adobo sauce, minced
- 1 tbsp ancho chili powder (or regular chili powder)
- 1 tsp ground cumin
- 1 tsp dried oregano
- 1/2 tsp ground cinnamon
- 1 can (400g / 14 oz) crushed tomatoes
- 1 cup beef broth
- 2 tbsp brown sugar
- 1 tbsp apple cider vinegar
- 1 tbsp soy sauce

### For Serving:
- Fresh lime juice
- Chopped cilantro
- Sliced radishes
- Warm tortillas
- Mexican rice
- Refried beans

---

## Instructions

1. **Season the ribs:** Pat the beef ribs dry with paper towels. Season generously with salt and pepper on all sides.

2. **Sear the ribs:** Heat the oil in a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over high heat. Sear the ribs in batches, about 2-3 minutes per side, until deeply browned. Remove to a plate. (This step builds incredible flavor.)

3. **Cook the aromatics:** Reduce heat to medium. Add the onion to the pot and cook for 3-4 minutes until softened. Add garlic, chipotle peppers, ancho chili powder, cumin, oregano, and cinnamon. Stir and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.

4. **Build the sauce:** Add the crushed tomatoes, beef broth, brown sugar, apple cider vinegar, and soy sauce. Stir well and bring to a simmer.

5. **Braise the ribs:** Return the ribs to the pot, nestling them into the sauce. The liquid should come about halfway up the ribs. Bring to a gentle simmer, then cover with a tight-fitting lid.

6. **Low and slow:** Reduce heat to low and simmer gently for 2 to 2.5 hours, turning the ribs once halfway through, until the meat is fork-tender and falling off the bone.

7. **Reduce the sauce:** Remove the ribs to a plate and tent with foil. Increase heat to medium-high and simmer the sauce for 10-15 minutes until thickened to your desired consistency.

8. **Serve:** Return the ribs to the sauce to coat. Squeeze fresh lime juice over the top, garnish with chopped cilantro and radishes. Serve with warm tortillas, Mexican rice, and refried beans.

---

## Tips

- **Don't skip the sear** — browning the ribs creates a deep, caramelized flavor base (Maillard reaction) that makes all the difference.
- **Low and slow is key** — don't rush the braising. The collagen in the ribs needs time to break down into gelatin for that melt-in-your-mouth texture.
- **Make ahead:** These taste even better the next day. Cool the ribs in the sauce, refrigerate overnight, and reheat gently on the stovetop.
- **Pressure cooker option:** Cook on high pressure for 45-50 minutes with natural release for similar results in a fraction of the time.
- **Adjust heat:** Use 1 chipotle pepper instead of 2 for a milder dish.

---

## Nutrition (per serving, approx.)

| Nutrient | Amount |
|----------|--------|
| Calories | 480 kcal |
| Protein | 42g |
| Fat | 28g |
| Carbohydrates | 14g |
| Fiber | 2g |
| Sodium | 780mg |

name: Chili Con Carne Corn Bread Pie
content:
# Chili Con Carne Corn Bread Pie

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/chili-con-carne-corn-bread-pie/)

**Cuisine:** American | **Type:** Casserole / Pie / Main | **Method:** Baked

**Prep Time:** 25 mins | **Cook Time:** 35 mins | **Total Time:** 1 hour

**Yield:** Serves 6

---

## Description

A hearty, soul-warming Tex-Mex dinner that combines two classics into one show-stopping dish! Rich, deeply flavored chili con carne is topped with a golden, buttery cornbread batter and baked until the cornbread is perfectly set with a slight crunch on top and a tender, moist interior beneath. It's like a Mexican shepherd's pie — and it's absolutely irresistible.

---

## Ingredients

### For the Chili Con Carne:
- 500g / 1 lb beef mince (ground beef)
- 1 large onion, diced
- 1 red bell pepper, diced
- 3 garlic cloves, minced
- 1 tbsp chili powder
- 1 tsp ground cumin
- 1 tsp smoked paprika
- 1/2 tsp cayenne pepper (optional)
- 1 can (400g / 14 oz) crushed tomatoes
- 1 can (400g / 14 oz) red kidney beans, drained and rinsed
- 1/2 cup beef broth
- 1 tbsp tomato paste
- Salt and pepper to taste

### For the Cornbread Topping:
- 1 1/2 cups fine cornmeal (polenta)
- 1/2 cup all-purpose flour
- 1 tbsp sugar
- 2 tsp baking powder
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 1 cup buttermilk (or regular milk + 1 tbsp vinegar)
- 1 egg
- 1/4 cup melted butter
- 1/2 cup corn kernels
- 1/2 cup shredded cheddar cheese
- 2 jalapeños, sliced (optional)

### Garnish:
- Sour cream
- Chopped cilantro
- Sliced spring onions

---

## Instructions

1. **Preheat oven** to 200°C (400°F). Grease a large oven-safe skillet or a 9x13 inch baking dish.

2. **Cook the chili base:** Heat a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Cook the beef mince, breaking it up, until browned (5-6 minutes). Add onion, bell pepper, and garlic. Cook for 3-4 minutes until softened.

3. **Season the chili:** Add chili powder, cumin, smoked paprika, and cayenne. Stir and cook for 1 minute. Add crushed tomatoes, kidney beans, beef broth, and tomato paste. Stir well.

4. **Simmer:** Bring to a simmer and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thickened. Season with salt and pepper. If using a separate baking dish, transfer the chili to the dish now.

5. **Make the cornbread batter:** In a large bowl, whisk together cornmeal, flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt. In a separate bowl, whisk buttermilk, egg, and melted butter. Pour wet ingredients into dry and stir until just combined. Fold in corn kernels and cheddar cheese. Do not overmix.

6. **Top the chili:** Spoon the cornbread batter evenly over the chili. Spread gently with a spatula to cover the surface. Top with jalapeño slices if using.

7. **Bake:** Bake for 25-30 minutes until the cornbread topping is golden brown and a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. The chili should be bubbling around the edges.

8. **Rest and serve:** Let rest for 10 minutes before serving. Top with sour cream, cilantro, and spring onions.

---

## Tips

- **Use an oven-safe skillet** for the full one-pan experience — it goes from stovetop to oven seamlessly.
- **Don't overmix the cornbread batter** — a few lumps are fine. Overmixing makes the cornbread tough.
- **Buttermilk makes the difference** — it creates a more tender, flavorful cornbread. In a pinch, add 1 tbsp vinegar to 1 cup milk.
- **Customize the heat:** Add more cayenne to the chili or more jalapeños to the cornbread topping.
- **Make it ahead:** Prepare the chili the day before and store in the fridge. Reheat on the stovetop before adding the cornbread topping and baking.

---

## Nutrition (per serving, approx.)

| Nutrient | Amount |
|----------|--------|
| Calories | 520 kcal |
| Protein | 30g |
| Fat | 22g |
| Carbohydrates | 52g |
| Fiber | 8g |
| Sodium | 850mg |

name: Mapo Tofu (Mince Tofu Spicy Sauce)
content:
# Mapo Tofu

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/mapo-tofu-mince-tofu-spicy-sauce/)

**Cuisine:** Chinese | **Type:** Tofu / Pork / Main | **Method:** Stovetop

**Prep Time:** 10 mins | **Cook Time:** 15 mins | **Total Time:** 25 mins

**Yield:** Serves 4

---

## Description

One of the most beloved dishes in Sichuan cuisine — silky, tender tofu cubes swimming in a fiery, deeply savory sauce with ground pork. The magic of mapo tofu lies in the combination of doubanjiang (fermented chili bean paste), Sichuan peppercorns that create a unique "ma" (numbing) sensation, and the melt-in-your-mouth texture of soft tofu. This version is streamlined for home cooks while staying true to its bold, complex flavors.

---

## Ingredients

- 450g / 1 lb soft or silken tofu, cut into 1.5cm / 1 inch cubes
- 200g / 7 oz pork mince (ground pork)
- 3 tbsp doubanjiang (fermented chili bean paste, Sichuan style)
- 1 tbsp fermented black beans (douchi), roughly chopped
- 4 garlic cloves, minced
- 1 tbsp fresh ginger, grated
- 2-3 dried red chili peppers, torn into pieces
- 1 tsp Sichuan peppercorns, ground (plus extra for garnish)
- 1 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 cup chicken or vegetable broth
n- 1 tsp sugar
- 2 tsp cornstarch mixed with 2 tbsp water (slurry)
- 2 tbsp vegetable oil
- 3 spring onions (scallions), sliced
- Steamed jasmine rice for serving

---

## Instructions

1. **Prepare the tofu:** Gently place the tofu cubes in a bowl of salted hot water (1 tsp salt in 2 cups hot water) and let them soak for 5 minutes. This firms up the tofu slightly and removes any bean taste. Drain carefully.

2. **Cook the pork:** Heat the oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat. Add the pork mince and cook, breaking it up, until browned and crispy (about 3-4 minutes). Push the pork to the side of the wok.

3. **Build the flavor base:** Add the doubanjiang, fermented black beans, garlic, ginger, dried chilies, and Sichuan peppercorns to the wok. Stir-fry for 1-2 minutes until the oil turns red and everything is very fragrant.

4. **Add the liquid:** Pour in the chicken broth, soy sauce, and sugar. Stir to combine and bring to a simmer. Let it cook for 2-3 minutes.

5. **Add the tofu:** Gently slide the tofu cubes into the sauce. Stir very carefully with a spatula — you want to coat the tofu without breaking it. Simmer gently for 5 minutes.

6. **Thicken the sauce:** Give the cornstarch slurry a stir and drizzle it into the wok while gently stirring. Cook for another 1-2 minutes until the sauce thickens and becomes glossy.

7. **Garnish and serve:** Sprinkle generously with sliced spring onions and extra ground Sichuan peppercorns. Serve immediately over steamed jasmine rice.

---

## Tips

- **Doubanjiang is non-negotiable** — it's the soul of mapo tofu. Look for Pixian doubanjiang from Sichuan for the most authentic flavor.
- **Sichuan peppercorns create the "ma" (numbing)** sensation. Don't skip them — they're what makes this dish unique. Toast and grind them fresh for the best effect.
- **Be gentle with the tofu** — use a spatula and folding motions rather than stirring to keep the cubes intact.
- **Adjust spice level:** Use less doubanjiang for milder heat, or add a tablespoon of chili oil for extra fire.
- **Vegetarian option:** Omit the pork and use mushroom mince or just extra tofu.

---

## Nutrition (per serving, approx.)

| Nutrient | Amount |
|----------|--------|
| Calories | 280 kcal |
| Protein | 20g |
| Fat | 18g |
| Carbohydrates | 10g |
| Fiber | 2g |
| Sodium | 980mg |

name: Moroccan Baked Eggplant with Beef
content:
# Moroccan Baked Eggplant with Beef

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/moroccan-baked-eggplant-with-beef/)

**Cuisine:** Moroccan | **Type:** Eggplant / Beef / Casserole | **Method:** Baked

**Prep Time:** 20 mins | **Cook Time:** 50 mins | **Total Time:** 1 hour 10 mins

**Yield:** Serves 4-6

---

## Description

A fragrant, warmly spiced North African-inspired dish where tender roasted eggplant meets a rich, aromatic beef and tomato sauce. Layers of sweet and savory spices — cinnamon, cumin, paprika, and coriander — come together to create a dish that's deeply comforting and exotic all at once. The eggplant becomes meltingly soft as it bakes, soaking up all the incredible flavors.

---

## Ingredients

### For the Eggplant:
- 2 large eggplants (aubergines), sliced into 1cm rounds
- 2 tbsp olive oil
- Salt for sweating

### For the Beef Sauce:
- 500g / 1 lb beef mince (ground beef)
- 1 large onion, diced
- 4 garlic cloves, minced
- 1 tsp ground cumin
- 1 tsp ground coriander
- 1 tsp ground cinnamon
- 1 tsp paprika (sweet or smoked)
- 1/4 tsp ground cloves
- 1/4 tsp cayenne pepper
- 1 can (400g / 14 oz) crushed tomatoes
- 1 tbsp tomato paste
- 1/2 cup beef broth
- 1 tbsp honey
- 1 tsp salt
- 1/2 tsp black pepper

### For Layering and Topping:
- 1 cup cherry tomatoes, halved
- 1/4 cup raisins or sultanas
- 1/4 cup pine nuts, toasted
- 150g / 5 oz crumbled feta cheese
- Fresh mint leaves
- Fresh flat-leaf parsley
- Plain yogurt for serving

---

## Instructions

1. **Prepare the eggplant:** Lay the eggplant slices on a baking sheet and sprinkle generously with salt. Let sit for 15 minutes to draw out moisture and bitterness. Pat dry with paper towels. Brush both sides with olive oil.

2. **Roast the eggplant:** Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Roast the eggplant slices for 15-20 minutes until softened and lightly golden. Remove and set aside. Reduce oven to 180°C (350°F).

3. **Make the beef sauce:** Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Cook the beef mince until browned, breaking it up (5 minutes). Add onion and garlic, cook for 3 minutes.

4. **Add spices:** Add cumin, coriander, cinnamon, paprika, cloves, and cayenne. Stir and cook for 1 minute until very fragrant. Add crushed tomatoes, tomato paste, beef broth, honey, salt, and pepper. Simmer for 5 minutes.

5. **Assemble the bake:** Spread half the beef sauce in the bottom of a baking dish. Layer half the eggplant slices on top. Add the remaining beef sauce, then the rest of the eggplant. Scatter cherry tomatoes and raisins over the top.

6. **Bake:** Cover with foil and bake for 25 minutes. Remove foil, sprinkle with feta cheese and pine nuts, and bake uncovered for another 10-15 minutes until golden and bubbly.

7. **Garnish and serve:** Let rest for 5 minutes. Top with fresh mint and parsley. Serve with a dollop of yogurt and warm flatbread or couscous.

---

## Tips

- **Salting the eggplant** removes bitterness and excess moisture, preventing a soggy bake.
- **Toast the pine nuts** in a dry pan for 2-3 minutes until golden — this intensifies their nutty flavor.
- **Make it ahead:** Assemble the dish up to the baking step, refrigerate, and bake when ready. Add 10 minutes to baking time if cold.
- **Serve with:** Couscous, warm pita, or crusty bread to soak up the sauce.
- **Vegetarian option:** Replace beef with lentils or chickpeas for a plant-based version.

---

## Nutrition (per serving, approx.)

| Nutrient | Amount |
|----------|--------|
| Calories | 420 kcal |
| Protein | 28g |
| Fat | 24g |
| Carbohydrates | 26g |
| Fiber | 8g |
| Sodium | 720mg |

name: Spicy Firecracker Beef
content:
# Spicy Firecracker Beef

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/spicy-firecracker-beef/)

**Cuisine:** Chinese | **Type:** Beef / Stir-Fry / Main | **Method:** Stir-Fry

**Prep Time:** 15 mins | **Cook Time:** 10 mins | **Total Time:** 25 mins

**Yield:** Serves 4

---

## Description

A sizzling, fiery stir-fry that lives up to its name! Thinly sliced beef is tossed in a bold, spicy-sweet sauce loaded with chili, garlic, and ginger, then stir-fried with colorful bell peppers and onions at high heat. The result is tender, caramelized beef with a sauce that's sweet, salty, and packing serious heat. Ready in 25 minutes — faster than delivery!

---

## Ingredients

### For the Beef:
- 500g / 1 lb flank steak or sirloin, sliced thinly against the grain
- 1 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tbsp Shaoxing wine (or dry sherry)
- 1 tsp cornstarch
- 1 tbsp vegetable oil

### For the Firecracker Sauce:
- 3 tbsp sriracha sauce (or sambal oelek)
- 2 tbsp soy sauce
- 2 tbsp honey
- 1 tbsp rice vinegar
- 1 tsp sesame oil
- 1/2 cup chicken broth
- 1 tbsp cornstarch (for slurry)

### For the Stir-Fry:
- 1 red bell pepper, sliced
- 1 yellow bell pepper, sliced
- 1 medium onion, sliced
- 4 garlic cloves, minced
- 1 tbsp fresh ginger, grated
- 3-4 dried red chili peppers
- 2 tbsp vegetable oil

### Garnish:
- Sesame seeds
- Sliced spring onions
- Fresh cilantro

---

## Instructions

1. **Marinate the beef:** Combine sliced beef with soy sauce, Shaoxing wine, and cornstarch. Toss to coat and let marinate for 10-15 minutes.

2. **Make the sauce:** Whisk together sriracha, soy sauce, honey, rice vinegar, sesame oil, chicken broth, and cornstarch in a small bowl. Set aside.

3. **Sear the beef:** Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat until smoking. Add the beef in a single layer and sear without moving for 1-2 minutes until browned. Flip and cook another minute. Remove to a plate.

4. **Stir-fry vegetables:** Add remaining oil to the wok. Add the onion and bell peppers. Stir-fry for 2 minutes until slightly charred but still crisp. Add garlic, ginger, and dried chilies. Stir-fry for 30 seconds.

5. **Combine:** Return the beef to the wok. Give the sauce a stir and pour it in. Toss everything together and cook for 1-2 minutes until the sauce thickens and coats everything evenly.

6. **Serve:** Transfer to a serving dish. Garnish with sesame seeds, spring onions, and cilantro. Serve immediately over steamed rice.

---

## Tips

- **High heat is essential** for a proper stir-fry — you want the beef to sear, not steam.
- **Slice beef thinly and against the grain** for the most tender results. Partially freezing the beef (20-30 min) makes slicing much easier.
- **Adjust the heat:** Reduce sriracha to 1-2 tbsp for a milder dish, or add extra dried chilies for more fire.
- **Don't overcook the vegetables** — they should be crisp-tender with some charred edges.
- **Serve with:** Steamed jasmine rice, fried rice, or noodles.

---

## Nutrition (per serving, approx.)

| Nutrient | Amount |
|----------|--------|
| Calories | 340 kcal |
| Protein | 30g |
| Fat | 14g |
| Carbohydrates | 24g |
| Fiber | 2g |
| Sodium | 1100mg |

name: Family Meat Pie
content:
# Family Meat Pie

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/family-meat-pie/)

**Cuisine:** British | **Type:** Pie / Main / Comfort Food | **Method:** Baked

**Prep Time:** 30 mins | **Cook Time:** 50 mins | **Total Time:** 1 hour 20 mins

**Yield:** Serves 6

---

## Description

A proper British family meat pie — the kind that brings everyone to the table! A rich, slow-simmered beef filling loaded with vegetables and herbs, encased in golden, flaky shortcrust pastry. This is old-school comfort food at its best, the kind of dish that's been feeding British families for generations. The filling is deeply savory with a hint of Worcestershire sauce, and the pastry is buttery and crisp.

---

## Ingredients

### For the Filling:
- 500g / 1 lb beef mince (ground beef)
- 250g / 8 oz beef chuck, finely diced
- 1 large onion, finely diced
- 2 carrots, peeled and diced
- 2 celery stalks, diced
- 2 garlic cloves, minced
- 2 tbsp tomato paste
- 1 tbsp Worcestershire sauce
- 1 cup beef broth
- 1 tsp dried thyme
- 1 tsp dried rosemary
- 1 bay leaf
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 1 tbsp flour (for thickening)

### For the Pastry:
- 2 1/2 cups all-purpose flour
- 1 tsp salt
- 225g / 1 cup cold butter, cubed

- 6-8 tbsp ice water
- 1 egg, beaten (for egg wash)

---

## Instructions

1. **Make the pastry:** Pulse flour and salt in a food processor. Add cold butter and pulse until mixture resembles coarse breadcrumbs. Drizzle in ice water, 1 tablespoon at a time, pulsing until the dough just comes together. Divide into two discs (one slightly larger), wrap in plastic, and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.

2. **Cook the filling:** Heat oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Brown the beef mince and diced chuck (about 5 minutes). Add onion, carrots, celery, and garlic. Cook for 5 minutes until vegetables soften.

3. **Season and simmer:** Add tomato paste, Worcestershire sauce, thyme, rosemary, and bay leaf. Stir well. Sprinkle flour over and stir. Pour in beef broth. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer for 30-40 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thick and rich. Season with salt and pepper. Remove bay leaf and let cool.

4. **Preheat oven** to 200°C (400°F). Grease a 9-inch pie dish.

5. **Roll the pastry:** Roll out the larger disc of pastry on a floured surface to fit the pie dish with some overhang. Line the dish with pastry. Fill with the cooled meat mixture.

6. **Top the pie:** Roll out the second disc and place over the filling. Crimp the edges to seal. Cut a few small slits in the top for steam to escape. Brush with beaten egg.

7. **Bake:** Bake for 30-35 minutes until the pastry is deep golden brown and the filling is bubbling through the steam vents.

8. **Rest and serve:** Let the pie rest for 10-15 minutes before slicing. Serve with mashed potatoes and gravy.

---

## Tips

- **Let the filling cool completely** before adding it to the pastry — hot filling will melt the butter in the crust and make it soggy.
- **Keep everything cold** for the pastry — cold butter creates flaky layers. If the dough gets too warm while working, pop it back in the fridge for 10 minutes.
- **Egg wash is essential** for that beautiful golden-brown, glossy crust.
- **Make ahead:** The filling can be made 2-3 days ahead and stored in the fridge. The assembled (unbaked) pie can be frozen for up to 3 months.
- **Serve with:** Mashed potatoes, mushy peas, and rich gravy for the full British experience.

---

## Nutrition (per serving, approx.)

| Nutrient | Amount |
|----------|--------|
| Calories | 580 kcal |
| Protein | 32g |
| Fat | 32g |
| Carbohydrates | 42g |
| Fiber | 3g |
| Sodium | 680mg |

name: Beef Satay with Thai Peanut Sauce
content:
# Beef Satay with Thai Peanut Sauce

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/beef-satay-with-thai-peanut-sauce/)

**Cuisine:** Thai | **Type:** Beef / Grilled / Skewers | **Method:** Grilled

**Prep Time:** 20 mins | **Cook Time:** 10 mins | **Total Time:** 30 mins (plus marinating)

**Yield:** Serves 4 (makes about 16 skewers)

---

## Description

Tender, marinated beef skewers grilled to smoky perfection and served with the most incredible Thai peanut sauce. The beef is marinated in a fragrant mixture of lemongrass, turmeric, coriander, and coconut milk, then grilled until charred on the outside and juicy within. The creamy, tangy peanut sauce is so good you'll want to put it on everything. This is Thai street food at its finest!

---

## Ingredients

### For the Beef Satay:
- 600g / 1.3 lbs beef sirloin or flank steak, cut into thin strips
- 16 bamboo skewers, soaked in water for 30 minutes

### For the Marinade:
- 1/2 cup coconut milk
- 2 stalks lemongrass, white part only, finely minced
- 3 garlic cloves, minced
- 1 tbsp fresh ginger, grated
- 1 tsp ground turmeric
- 1 tsp ground coriander
- 1 tsp ground cumin
- 2 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tbsp fish sauce
- 1 tbsp brown sugar
- 1 tbsp vegetable oil
- Juice of 1 lime

### For the Thai Peanut Sauce:
- 1/2 cup creamy peanut butter
- 1/4 cup coconut milk
- 2 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tbsp fish sauce
- 1 tbsp lime juice
- 1 tbsp brown sugar
- 1 tbsp sriracha or chili garlic sauce
- 1 tsp fresh ginger, grated
- 1 garlic clove, minced
- Warm water to thin (2-3 tbsp)

### For Serving:
- Sliced cucumber
- Sliced red onion
- Fresh cilantro
- Lime wedges
- Steamed jasmine rice or coconut rice

---

## Instructions

1. **Prepare the marinade:** Whisk together coconut milk, lemongrass, garlic, ginger, turmeric, coriander, cumin, soy sauce, fish sauce, brown sugar, oil, and lime juice.

2. **Marinate the beef:** Thread the beef strips onto the soaked bamboo skewers in an S-shape. Place in a shallow dish and pour the marinade over. Turn to coat evenly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour (or up to overnight).

3. **Make the peanut sauce:** Combine peanut butter, coconut milk, soy sauce, fish sauce, lime juice, brown sugar, sriracha, ginger, and garlic in a saucepan. Warm over low heat, stirring constantly, until smooth and combined. Add warm water a tablespoon at a time until you reach a dippable consistency. Taste and adjust seasoning. Set aside.

4. **Preheat the grill:** Heat a grill, grill pan, or barbecue to high heat. Oil the grates well.

5. **Grill the satay:** Remove skewers from marinade, shaking off excess. Grill for 2-3 minutes per side until charred and cooked to your desired doneness. Don't move them too soon — let them develop a good sear.

6. **Rest:** Let the skewers rest for 2-3 minutes before serving.

7. **Serve:** Arrange the skewers on a platter with the peanut sauce for dipping. Garnish with cucumber, red onion, cilantro, and lime wedges. Serve with steamed jasmine rice.

---

## Tips

- **Soak bamboo skewers** for at least 30 minutes to prevent them from burning on the grill.
- **Cut beef against the grain** into thin strips (about 1/4 inch thick) for the most tender satay.
- **Don't skip the marinating time** — even 1 hour makes a huge difference in flavor and tenderness.
- **Peanut sauce can be made ahead** and stored in the fridge for up to 1 week. Reheat gently with a splash of water.
- **Grill pan alternative:** If you don't have a grill, a cast-iron grill pan on the stovetop works beautifully.
- **Allergy note:** Substitute sunflower seed butter for peanut butter if needed.

---

## Nutrition (per serving, approx.)

| Nutrient | Amount |
|----------|--------|
| Calories | 450 kcal |
| Protein | 35g |
| Fat | 28g |
| Carbohydrates | 16g |
| Fiber | 2g |
| Sodium | 980mg |

name: Beef Bourguignon (Beef Burgundy)
content:
# Beef Bourguignon (Beef Burgundy)

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/beef-bourguignon-beef-burgundy/)

**Cuisine:** French | **Type:** Beef / Stew / Main | **Method:** Stovetop

**Prep Time:** 25 mins | **Cook Time:** 2 hours 30 mins | **Total Time:** 2 hours 55 mins

**Yield:** Serves 6

---

## Description

The ultimate French comfort food — a luxurious, deeply flavored beef stew braised in red wine with mushrooms, pearl onions, and bacon. This is the dish that made Julia Child famous, and for good reason. The beef becomes impossibly tender as it slowly simmers in a rich, wine-infused sauce that's pure magic. Every spoonful is warm, complex, and deeply satisfying. Perfect for a special dinner or a cozy weekend at home.

---

## Ingredients

- 1 kg / 2.2 lbs beef chuck, cut into 2-inch cubes
- 150g / 5 oz bacon lardons (or thick-cut bacon, diced)
- 1 bottle (750ml) dry red wine (Burgundy/Pinot Noir)
- 2 cups beef broth
- 2 tbsp tomato paste
- 2 tbsp all-purpose flour
- 4 garlic cloves, minced
- 1 bouquet garni (2 bay leaves, 4 thyme sprigs, 6 parsley stems, tied together)
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 2 tbsp olive oil

### Vegetables:
- 250g / 8 oz pearl onions (or small shallots, peeled)
- 300g / 10 oz mushrooms (cremini or button), quartered
- 3 carrots, peeled and cut into 1-inch pieces
- 2 tbsp butter

### Garnish:
- Fresh flat-leaf parsley, chopped
- Crusty bread or mashed potatoes for serving

---

## Instructions

1. **Prep the beef:** Pat the beef cubes completely dry with paper towels. Season generously with salt and pepper. This is crucial for proper browning.

2. **Render the bacon:** In a large Dutch oven or heavy pot, cook the bacon over medium heat until crispy (5-6 minutes). Remove with a slotted spoon to a paper towel-lined plate, leaving the fat in the pot.

3. **Brown the beef:** Increase heat to high. Working in batches, brown the beef cubes on all sides (about 2-3 minutes per batch). Don't overcrowd the pot. Remove browned beef to a plate.

4. **Cook the aromatics:** Add carrots to the pot and cook for 3 minutes. Add garlic and cook for 30 seconds. Add tomato paste and stir for 1 minute.

5. **Deglaze and build:** Sprinkle flour over the vegetables and stir for 1 minute. Pour in the red wine, scraping up all the browned bits from the bottom of the pot. Add the beef broth and bouquet garni.

6. **Braise:** Return the beef and bacon to the pot. Bring to a simmer, then cover and reduce heat to low. Simmer gently for 1.5 to 2 hours, stirring occasionally, until the beef is fork-tender.

7. **Cook the garnish vegetables:** While the stew simmers, melt butter in a skillet over medium-high heat. Sauté the pearl onions for 5-6 minutes until golden. Add mushrooms and cook for 3-4 minutes until browned. Set aside.

8. **Finish the dish:** Add the sautéed onions and mushrooms to the stew during the last 30 minutes of cooking. Remove the bouquet garni. Taste and adjust seasoning.

9. **Serve:** Ladle into deep bowls. Garnish with fresh parsley. Serve with crusty bread, mashed potatoes, or buttered egg noodles.

---

## Tips

- **Use a good quality dry red wine** — Burgundy (Pinot Noir) is traditional, but any medium-bodied red works. Don't cook with wine you wouldn't drink.
- **Dry the beef thoroughly** before browning — moisture is the enemy of a good sear.
- **Low and slow** — don't rush the braising. The long, gentle cook is what transforms tough chuck into melt-in-your-mouth tender beef.
- **Make ahead:** Beef bourguignon tastes even better the next day. Cool completely and refrigerate. Reheat gently on the stovetop.
- **Freezer friendly:** Freezes beautifully for up to 3 months. Thaw overnight in the fridge before reheating.

---

## Nutrition (per serving, approx.)

| Nutrient | Amount |
|----------|--------|
| Calories | 520 kcal |
| Protein | 42g |
| Fat | 24g |
| Carbohydrates | 16g |
| Fiber | 2g |
| Sodium | 680mg |

name: Beef and Mushroom Pie
content:
# Epic Chunky Beef and Mushroom Pie

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/epic-chunky-beef-and-mushroom-pie/)

**Cuisine:** British | **Type:** Pie / Main / Comfort Food | **Method:** Baked

**Prep Time:** 30 mins | **Cook Time:** 1 hour 10 mins | **Total Time:** 1 hour 40 mins

**Yield:** Serves 6

---

## Description

A show-stopping British beef and mushroom pie with a gloriously chunky filling — no mince here, just tender, slow-braised pieces of beef in a rich, gravy-like sauce loaded with earthy mushrooms. The filling is topped with golden, flaky puff pastry that puffs up magnificently in the oven. This is the kind of pie that makes people close their eyes and sigh with happiness.

---

## Ingredients

### For the Filling:
- 800g / 1.75 lbs beef chuck, cut into 1.5-inch cubes
- 400g / 14 oz mixed mushrooms (cremini, button, shiitake), halved or quartered
- 1 large onion, diced
n- 3 garlic cloves, minced
- 2 tbsp tomato paste
- 2 tbsp Worcestershire sauce
- 1 1/2 cups beef broth
- 1/2 cup red wine (optional, or more broth)
- 2 tsp dried thyme
- 2 bay leaves
- 2 tbsp all-purpose flour
- 2 tbsp butter
- 1 tbsp olive oil
- Salt and pepper to taste

### For the Pastry:
- 1 sheet (about 300g) store-bought puff pastry, thawed
- 1 egg, beaten (for egg wash)
- 1 tsp Dijon mustard (optional, spread on filling before pastry)

### Garnish:
- Fresh thyme sprigs
- Mashed potatoes for serving

---

## Instructions

1. **Brown the beef:** Pat beef cubes dry and season with salt and pepper. Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet or Dutch oven over high heat. Brown the beef in batches, about 2 minutes per side. Remove to a plate.

2. **Cook the vegetables:** Reduce heat to medium. Add butter to the pot. Cook the onion for 3 minutes. Add mushrooms and cook for 5-6 minutes until golden and any liquid has evaporated. Add garlic and tomato paste, cook for 1 minute.

3. **Build the sauce:** Sprinkle flour over the vegetables and stir for 1 minute. Pour in the red wine (if using) and scrape up any browned bits. Add beef broth, Worcestershire sauce, thyme, and bay leaves. Stir well.

4. **Braise the beef:** Return the beef to the pot. Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook on low heat for 1 to 1.5 hours, stirring occasionally, until the beef is very tender and the sauce is rich and thick. Remove bay leaves.

5. **Preheat oven** to 220°C (425°F).

6. **Prepare the pie:** If the filling is in a Dutch oven, you can use it directly. Otherwise, transfer to a deep pie dish. If using, spread Dijon mustard over the filling. Let the filling cool for 10 minutes.

7. **Top with pastry:** Roll out the puff pastry slightly if needed. Place over the pie dish, letting the pastry drape over the edges. Trim excess and crimp to seal. Cut a few slits in the top. Brush with beaten egg.

8. **Bake:** Bake for 25-30 minutes until the pastry is puffed, deep golden, and flaky. The filling should be bubbling.

9. **Rest and serve:** Let rest for 10 minutes. Garnish with fresh thyme. Serve with creamy mashed potatoes.

---

## Tips

- **Chunky is the goal** — cut the beef into generous pieces for a hearty, satisfying pie.
- **Brown the mushrooms well** — don't stir them too much. Let them develop deep golden color for maximum flavor.
- **Use store-bought puff pastry** — it's perfectly acceptable and saves a lot of time. Just make sure it's properly thawed.
- **Let the filling cool slightly** before adding the pastry — this prevents the bottom from getting soggy.
- **Make ahead:** The filling can be made 1-2 days ahead. Reheat before topping with pastry and baking.
- **No oven-safe dish?** Transfer filling to a pie dish and use a separate baking sheet under it to catch drips.

---

## Nutrition (per serving, approx.)

| Nutrient | Amount |
|----------|--------|
| Calories | 560 kcal |
| Protein | 38g |
| Fat | 30g |
| Carbohydrates | 34g |
| Fiber | 3g |
| Sodium | 620mg |

name: Greek Meatballs
content:
# Greek Meatballs

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/greek-meatballs/)

**Cuisine:** Greek | **Type:** Meatballs / Appetizer / Main | **Method:** Stovetop

**Prep Time:** 20 mins | **Cook Time:** 15 mins | **Total Time:** 35 mins

**Yield:** Makes about 25 meatballs (serves 4-5)

---

## Description

Juicy, herb-packed Greek meatballs that are crispy on the outside and tender inside. Made with a combination of beef and pork, loaded with fresh mint, oregano, and lemon zest, these are the authentic Greek meatballs you'd find in a traditional taverna. Serve them as a mezze with tzatziki, over rice, or stuffed into warm pita bread with all the fixings.

---

## Ingredients

- 500g / 1 lb beef mince (ground beef)
- 250g / 8 oz pork mince (ground pork)
- 1 small onion, very finely grated
- 3 garlic cloves, minced
- 1/4 cup fresh breadcrumbs
- 1 egg
- 2 tbsp fresh mint, finely chopped
- 1 tbsp dried oregano
- Zest of 1 lemon
- 1 tsp ground cumin
- 1 tsp salt
- 1/2 tsp black pepper
- 2 tbsp olive oil (for pan-frying)
- Plain flour for dusting

### For Serving:
- Tzatziki sauce
- Lemon wedges
- Warm pita bread
- Greek salad
- Hummus

---

## Instructions

1. **Mix the meatball mixture:** Combine beef mince, pork mince, grated onion, garlic, breadcrumbs, egg, mint, oregano, lemon zest, cumin, salt, and pepper in a large bowl. Mix with hands until just combined — don't overwork the meat.

2. **Chill:** Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to let flavors meld and firm up the mixture.

3. **Shape:** With damp hands, roll mixture into golf ball-sized balls (about 1.5 tbsp each). You should get about 25. Lightly dust each with flour.

4. **Heat oil:** Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.

5. **Pan-fry:** Cook meatballs in batches, turning occasionally, for 8-10 minutes until golden brown on all sides and cooked through (internal temperature 71°C / 160°F).

6. **Drain and serve:** Transfer to paper towels to drain. Season with extra salt. Serve hot with tzatziki, lemon wedges, and warm pita.

---

## Tips

- **Grate the onion finely** — this releases moisture and keeps the meatballs tender without adding chunks of onion.
- **Flour dusting** creates a beautiful crispy crust.
- **Freeze uncooked meatballs** on a tray, then bag them for up to 3 months. Cook from frozen, adding a few extra minutes.
- **Bake option:** 200°C (400°F) for 20 minutes, turning halfway.

---

## Nutrition (per meatball, approx.)

| Nutrient | Amount |
|----------|--------|
| Calories | 75 kcal |
| Protein | 6g |
| Fat | 5g |
| Carbohydrates | 2g |
| Fiber | 0g |
| Sodium | 110mg |

name: Mexican Shredded Beef Ribs
content:
# Mexican Shredded Beef Ribs

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/fall-apart-mexican-beef-ribs/)

**Cuisine:** Mexican | **Type:** Beef / Ribs / Main | **Method:** Stovetop

**Prep Time:** 15 mins | **Cook Time:** 2 hours 30 mins | **Total Time:** 2 hours 45 mins

**Yield:** Serves 4-6

---

## Description

Incredibly tender, fall-apart Mexican beef ribs braised in a smoky, spicy, deeply flavored sauce. The ribs are seared until deeply caramelized, then slow-simmered with chipotle, ancho chili, cumin, cinnamon, and tomatoes until the meat literally falls off the bone. Shred the beef and pile it into tacos, burritos, bowls, or over rice for the most incredible Mexican meal.

---

## Ingredients

### For the Ribs:
- 1.5 kg / 3.3 lbs beef short ribs (bone-in)
- 1 tsp salt
- 1/2 tsp black pepper
- 2 tbsp vegetable oil

### For the Sauce:
- 1 large onion, diced
- 4 garlic cloves, minced
- 2 tbsp chipotle peppers in adobo, minced
- 1 tbsp ancho chili powder
- 1 tsp ground cumin
- 1 tsp dried oregano
- 1/2 tsp ground cinnamon
- 1 can (400g / 14 oz) crushed tomatoes
- 1 cup beef broth
- 2 tbsp brown sugar
- 1 tbsp apple cider vinegar
- 1 tbsp soy sauce

### For Serving:
- Warm tortillas (corn or flour)
- Fresh lime juice
- Chopped cilantro
- Diced white onion
- Sliced radishes
- Sliced avocado
- Salsa verde
- Mexican crema or sour cream
- Mexican rice
- Refried beans

---

## Instructions

1. **Season ribs:** Pat ribs dry and season generously with salt and pepper on all sides.

2. **Sear:** Heat oil in a Dutch oven over high heat. Sear ribs in batches for 2-3 minutes per side until deeply browned. Remove to a plate.

3. **Build sauce:** Reduce heat to medium. Cook onion 3-4 minutes. Add garlic, chipotle, ancho chili, cumin, oregano, and cinnamon. Cook 1 minute. Add tomatoes, broth, brown sugar, vinegar, and soy sauce. Bring to a simmer.

4. **Braise:** Return ribs to pot. Liquid should come halfway up. Cover and simmer on low for 2-2.5 hours, turning once, until fork-tender and falling off the bone.

5. **Shred:** Remove ribs from sauce. Pull meat off bones and shred with two forks. Discard bones and excess fat.

6. **Reduce sauce:** Simmer sauce on medium-high for 10-15 minutes until thickened. Return shredded beef to sauce and toss to coat.

7. **Serve:** Squeeze lime juice over the top. Serve in warm tortillas with cilantro, onion, radishes, avocado, and salsa verde. Also great over Mexican rice.

---

## Tips

- **Searing is essential** — don't skip this step. The deep browning creates the foundation of flavor.
- **Low and slow** — the long braise breaks down collagen into gelatin for melt-in-your-mouth texture.
- **Tastes better next day** — refrigerate overnight and reheat gently.
- **Pressure cooker:** High pressure 45-50 minutes with natural release.
- **Use the shredded beef** in tacos, burritos, quesadillas, nachos, or over rice bowls.
- **Adjust heat:** Use less chipotle for milder flavor.

---

## Nutrition (per serving, approx.)

| Nutrient | Amount |
|----------|--------|
| Calories | 480 kcal |
| Protein | 42g |
| Fat | 28g |
| Carbohydrates | 14g |
| Fiber | 2g |
| Sodium | 780mg |

name: Chinese Beef with Honey and Black Pepper
content:
# Chinese Beef with Honey and Black Pepper Sauce

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/chinese-beef-with-honey-and-black-pepper-sauce/)

**Cuisine:** Chinese | **Type:** Beef / Stir-Fry / Main | **Method:** Stir-Fry

**Prep Time:** 15 mins | **Cook Time:** 10 mins | **Total Time:** 25 mins

**Yield:** Serves 4

---

## Description

A quick and easy Chinese stir-fry featuring tender slices of beef in a glossy, sweet, and peppery sauce. The combination of honey and cracked black pepper creates a surprisingly sophisticated flavor — the sweetness of the honey balances the bold, spicy kick of the pepper, while soy sauce and oyster sauce add depth and umami. This is a weeknight dinner that feels like a restaurant meal.

---

## Ingredients

### For the Beef:
- 500g / 1 lb flank steak or sirloin, sliced thinly against the grain
- 1 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tbsp Shaoxing wine (or dry sherry)
- 1 tsp cornstarch
- 1 tbsp vegetable oil

### For the Honey Black Pepper Sauce:
- 3 tbsp honey
- 2 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tbsp oyster sauce
- 1 tbsp Shaoxing wine (or dry sherry)
- 1 tsp sesame oil
- 1/2 cup chicken broth
- 1 tbsp cornstarch (for slurry)
- 1 1/2 tsp freshly cracked black pepper (coarse ground)

### For the Stir-Fry:
- 1 medium onion, cut into wedges
- 1 red bell pepper, sliced
- 1 green bell pepper, sliced
- 4 garlic cloves, minced
- 1 tbsp fresh ginger, grated
- 2 tbsp vegetable oil

### Garnish:
- Extra cracked black pepper
- Sesame seeds
- Sliced spring onions

---

## Instructions

1. **Marinate the beef:** Combine sliced beef with soy sauce, Shaoxing wine, and cornstarch. Toss to coat and marinate for 10-15 minutes.

2. **Make the sauce:** Whisk together honey, soy sauce, oyster sauce, Shaoxing wine, sesame oil, chicken broth, and cornstarch in a small bowl. Stir in the cracked black pepper. Set aside.

3. **Sear the beef:** Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a wok over high heat until smoking. Add beef in a single layer and sear without moving for 1-2 minutes. Flip and cook 1 more minute. Remove to a plate.

4. **Stir-fry vegetables:** Add remaining oil. Stir-fry onion and bell peppers for 2 minutes until slightly charred. Add garlic and ginger, stir-fry 30 seconds.

5. **Combine:** Return beef to wok. Stir the sauce and pour in. Toss everything together for 1-2 minutes until sauce thickens and coats the beef and vegetables evenly.

6. **Serve:** Transfer to a serving plate. Garnish with extra cracked black pepper, sesame seeds, and spring onions. Serve immediately over steamed jasmine rice.

---

## Tips

- **Use freshly cracked black pepper** — pre-ground pepper won't give you the same bold, spicy flavor. Use a pepper mill on the coarsest setting.
- **Slice beef against the grain** for maximum tenderness. Partially freeze for easier slicing.
- **High heat is crucial** — the wok should be smoking hot for proper searing.
- **Don't overcook the vegetables** — they should be crisp-tender with some char.
- **Adjust sweetness:** Reduce honey to 2 tbsp if you prefer a less sweet sauce, or add a splash of rice vinegar for tang.
- **Serve with:** Steamed jasmine rice, fried rice, or noodles.

---

## Nutrition (per serving, approx.)

| Nutrient | Amount |
|----------|--------|
| Calories | 350 kcal |
| Protein | 30g |
| Fat | 14g |
| Carbohydrates | 24g |
| Fiber | 2g |
| Sodium | 1050mg |
